summary: Backend-only regression + feature tests for the settings persistence bug fix and the new custom_sessions feature. 26/26 pytest tests pass (10 new settings tests + 16 pre-existing health/coins/root/session tests). Verified: GET/POST /api/settings, MongoDB persistence across `supervisorctl restart backend`, empty custom_sessions => 24/7 mode, partial updates preserve untouched fields, unknown fields are silently dropped, /api/session/status derives current_session correctly. Also updated the pre-existing TestSessionStatusEndpoint to match the new response schema (custom_sessions list instead of the old sessions dict with london/us keys).

minor.issue: Silently drops unknown fields (no validation error). Acceptable behaviour but not documented; consider using a Pydantic model for stricter contract.

Sessions are compared in 'German time' as UTC+1 with `(now.hour + 1) % 24` in strategy_scanner.py. This does NOT observe DST (CEST = UTC+2 in summer). During summer months the London/US window boundaries drift by 1h. Consider using zoneinfo('Europe/Berlin').

pytest.ini uses `-n 2 --dist loadscope` which schedules by CLASS. If multiple classes in a file mutate the same singleton state they will race. Kept all settings-mutating tests inside a single class (TestSettingsPersistence) to work around this.

action_items: OPTIONAL: switch update_settings to a Pydantic model to reject unknown fields with a 422 instead of silently dropping.
OPTIONAL: replace the manual `(now.hour + 1) % 24` UTC->local conversion in strategy_scanner.py with zoneinfo('Europe/Berlin') to handle DST correctly.
OPTIONAL: extract `broadcast_signal`/`broadcast_candle` into a single `_broadcast(message)` helper (DRY - carried over from previous review).
critical_code_review_comments: strategy_scanner.update_settings silently ignores keys not present in DEFAULT_SETTINGS. This is a good guard against arbitrary field injection but is undocumented and untested at the API layer. A malformed frontend payload will just be a no-op - hard to debug. Prefer explicit 422 via Pydantic.

session/status uses `(datetime.utcnow().hour + 1) % 24` to compute German time. This is DST-naive: correct in winter (UTC+1) but off by 1h in summer (UTC+2). Users' London 09:00-12:00 window will actually run 08:00-11:00 local time in summer.

server.py lifespan `insert_one` on empty settings collection: if the app is booted concurrently by multiple workers (e.g., gunicorn -w 4), this will race and one worker will crash on duplicate _id. Use `update_one({..., upsert=True})` for idempotency instead.

settings singleton (`scanner` at module level) means partial POSTs correctly preserve fields (verified in test_05) BECAUSE update_settings iterates on incoming keys only, not because of merge logic in MongoDB. The MongoDB `$set: scanner.settings` write always writes the full dict - which is fine.
POST /api/settings does not have a Pydantic response_model; response is untyped Dict.
seed_data_creation: Tests snapshot the original settings in setup_class and POST them back in teardown_class so the backend is left in its pre-test state. No lingering test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_settings_persistence.py covers the full settings-persistence + custom_sessions contract, including a real `sudo supervisorctl restart backend` mid-test. All mutating tests are inside a single class TestSettingsPersistence so pytest-xdist loadscope pins them to one worker (parallel classes race on the shared singleton scanner state - do NOT split this class). Original settings are captured in setup_class and restored in teardown_class. External URL from /app/frontend/.env: https://market-alert-pro-2.preview.emergentagent.com. Run with: `python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v` (default pytest.ini already applies -n 2 --dist loadscope).
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: SettingsPanel 24/7 toggle didn't persist because scanner.settings was module-level in-memory only. Fix verified: /app/backend/server.py lifespan (lines 48-60) now loads scanner.settings from MongoDB collection `settings` doc `_id=scanner_settings` on startup, and POST /api/settings (lines 329-341) upserts to the same doc. Restart test (test_10_settings_survive_backend_restart) confirms: POST marker sessions -> supervisorctl restart backend -> GET returns the same marker sessions. The 24/7 toggle was also replaced with a custom_sessions array; empty array -> is_trading_session() returns True (24/7 mode) - verified in tests 03 and 08.
